scope: abstract, introduction, design, evaluation, and conclusion
rounded SQLite hero claim terms: 6.4, 13.8, 8.8, 2.7, deadline, background
unsupported-claim scan for unnegated restored claims

abstract.text: On a Jetson AGX Thor Developer Kit, retained mounted artifacts show the intended spine: under secure-storage pressure, foreground SQLite p99 rises from 6.4~ms to 13.8~ms and incurs a deadline miss; AEGIS-Q's policy recovers p99 to 8.8~ms with zero misses while retaining 2.7~MB/s of background progress. The same evidence set exercises remount/tamper checks, generation/fault matrices, rekey fallback, TPM replay-after-advance, app-crash timing, and telemetry-to-FUSE throttling. The result is an evidence-first secure edge-storage prototype with explicit scope boundaries, not a claim of deployed-filesystem superiority.

conclusion.text: AEGIS-Q is an evidence-first secure edge-storage prototype: secure-storage pressure raises foreground SQLite p99 from 6.4~ms to 13.8~ms with one deadline miss; AEGIS-Q recovers 8.8~ms, zero misses, and 2.7~MB/s background progress. It does not claim direct NVMe-to-UVM DMA, eBPF/io\_uring completion bypass, GPU constant-time behavior, persistent PCR-bound freshness, power-loss certification, foreground AI p99 recovery, or portability.

design.text: QoS follows the SQLite contract in Figure~\ref{fig:first_page_qos}: secure-storage pressure raises AEGIS-Q p99 from 6.4~ms to 13.8~ms; classification recovers 8.8~ms, zero deadline misses, and 2.7~MB/s background progress, not foreground-inference p99 or completion-bypass control.

evaluation.text: The app-level result uses SQLite transaction latency as the foreground workload. Table~\ref{tab:qos_sqlite_recovery} is generated from the retained four-mode bundle. Mounted storage pressure raises SQLite p99 from 6.4~ms to 13.8~ms and causes one 10~ms deadline miss. AEGIS-Q marks foreground files latency-sensitive, throttles only elastic background writes, and recovers to 8.8~ms p99 with no misses at 2.7~MB/s. The simple controller reaches 8.2~ms p99 at 2.3~MB/s. This is SQLite recovery, not general AI-inference QoS.

introduction.text: Figure~\ref{fig:first_page_qos} shows the retained first-page pressure result. Mounted secure-storage pressure raises foreground SQLite p99 from 6.4~ms to 13.8~ms and introduces one 10~ms deadline miss. AEGIS-Q marks foreground files latency-sensitive, throttles only elastic background writes, and recovers to 8.8~ms p99 with zero misses while retaining 2.7~MB/s of background progress. This result motivates the paper's spine: policy must be visible at the secure-storage layer, but persistent format correctness must remain independent of the accelerator.
